item.title: Small but mighty: the human mycobiome as an immunopharmacological regulator of inflammation and autoimmunity.
item.description: International immunopharmacology - Review - The human mycobiome comprising commensal fungi such as Saccharomyces, Candida, Malassezia and others constitutes less than 0.1 percent of total gut microbes yet exerts outsized influence on host im...

item.title: Metabolic Profiling of Panax notoginseng Saponins in Rat Feces Under Colitis by UHPLC-QTOF-MS/MS.
item.description: Journal of separation science - The biotransformation of Panax notoginseng saponins (PNS) is pertinent to the effect of colitis mediated by gut microbiota, which has variant microbes and diversities in inflammatory bowel diseases...

item.title: GPX7 marks fibroblast-associated stromal-innate immune crosstalk in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Ulcerative colitis is sustained by stromal remodeling and innate immune activation. Whether serine-glycine-one-carbon metabolism contributes to fibroblast-macrophage crosstalk in ulcerative colitis...
